rationale: Both papers are in grocery retail and analyze how shopping conditions affect purchase composition/volume: the citing paper reports shifts in unplanned, healthy, and impulsive purchases, while the cited paper measures quantity and nutrition outcomes with impulse/healthfulness category heterogeneity. That topical and outcome-level overlap makes the cited paper a plausible substantive benchmark/background source rather than an obviously incidental mention, despite only DOI-level match evidence.
